summary: Tested push notification feature for True Joy Birthing app. All 18 backend API tests passed (100%). Found and fixed 3 bugs in frontend usePushNotifications.ts hook: 1) Wrong import (useAuth -> useAuthStore), 2) Double JSON.stringify on request body in register, 3) Double JSON.stringify in unregister. Backend APIs all working correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Backend push notification service (push_notifications.py) is well-implemented with proper error handling for DeviceNotRegisteredError, PushServerError, and PushTicketError
Push token validation uses correct Expo format check: ExponentPushToken[xxx]
Push tokens are stored in user document under push_tokens array with is_active flag, device_type, created_at, and last_verified timestamps
Integration with create_notification() properly sends push notifications when in-app notifications are created
push_logs collection stores delivery attempts for debugging purposes
Graceful failure handling: push notification failures don't break the main application flow

success_rate.frontend: N/A - Hook code fixed, requires physical device for full testing

context_for_next_testing_agent: Push notification feature fully tested. Backend APIs: POST /api/push/register, POST /api/push/unregister, GET /api/push/status all working. Frontend hook fixed - uses useAuthStore instead of useAuth, body passed as object not stringified. Expo push notifications require physical device or production build - test tokens work with API but won't deliver actual notifications.

features_tested.push_token_registration: PASS - POST /api/push/register accepts valid ExponentPushToken format
features_tested.push_token_unregistration: PASS - POST /api/push/unregister marks token as inactive
features_tested.push_status_check: PASS - GET /api/push/status returns has_push_enabled, active_devices, tokens array
features_tested.authentication_required: PASS - All endpoints require authentication (401 without)
features_tested.create_notification_integration: PASS - Push notifications sent when in-app notifications created
features_tested.graceful_failure_handling: PASS - System handles Expo service errors without crashing

endpoints_tested: POST /api/push/register - Register push token (200 on success, 400 on invalid format)
POST /api/push/unregister - Unregister push token (200 on success, 404 if not found)
GET /api/push/status - Get push notification status (has_push_enabled, active_devices, tokens)

bugs_fixed.issue: Wrong import: import { useAuth } from './useAuth' - useAuth hook doesn't exist
Double JSON.stringify in registerTokenWithBackend - body was stringified twice

notes: Actual push notification delivery requires: 1) Physical device with Expo app or production build, 2) Valid Expo push token from device registration. Test tokens (ExponentPushToken[test_xxx]) are accepted by API but won't deliver.
